annotations.label: Linear differential operator

anchor.snippet: an order-

annotations.note: Mathlib has iterated derivatives and `Derivation` but does not define an abstract "order-k linear differential operator with smooth coefficient functions" as a single concept.

annotations.label: Total symbol

anchor.snippet: is called the total symbol of P

annotations.note: No total/full symbol of a differential operator is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Principal symbol

anchor.snippet: is called the principal symbol of P

annotations.note: Grep for `principalSymbol`/`principal symbol` returns no Mathlib hits.

annotations.label: Differential operator between vector bundles

anchor.snippet: is a differential operator of order

annotations.note: Mathlib has vector bundles but no notion of differential operator between sections of vector bundles.

annotations.label: Principal symbol as symmetric tensor

anchor.snippet: This symmetric tensor is known as the principal symbol

annotations.note: No principal-symbol-as-symmetric-tensor construction exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fourier multiplier representation

anchor.snippet: This exhibits P as a Fourier multiplier

annotations.note: Mathlib has Fourier transforms but no Fourier-multiplier characterization of differential operators.

annotations.label: Elliptic operator

anchor.snippet: is elliptic if its symbol is invertible

annotations.note: No `EllipticOperator`/elliptic-operator definition appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic operators are Fredholm

anchor.snippet: P is a Fredholm operator

annotations.note: Mathlib has Fredholm-alternative material (`FredholmAlternative.lean`) but no statement that elliptic operators are Fredholm.

annotations.label: Wirtinger derivatives

anchor.snippet: Use is made of the Wirtinger derivatives

annotations.note: Grep for `Wirtinger` finds no Mathlib file; only Cauchy–Riemann section in `Analysis/Complex/Conformal.lean`.

annotations.label: Del operator

anchor.snippet: The differential operator del , also called nabla

annotations.note: The nabla/∇ operator (gradient of a scalar function) is `gradient` in `Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.Gradient.Basic`.

annotations.label: Adjoint of an operator

anchor.snippet: the adjoint of this operator is defined as the operator

annotations.note: The Hilbert-space adjoint of a (continuous) linear operator is `ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint`; unbounded version is `LinearPMap.adjoint`.

annotations.label: Scalar product on square-integrable functions

anchor.snippet: the scalar product is defined by

annotations.note: The L² inner product `⟪f,g⟫ = ∫ ⟪f a, g a⟫ ∂μ` is defined in `Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Function.L2Space`.

annotations.label: Formal adjoint

anchor.snippet: it is called the formal adjoint of T

annotations.note: `LinearPMap.IsFormalAdjoint` defines T as a formal adjoint of S via the bilinear identity ⟪T x, y⟫ = ⟪x, S y⟫.

annotations.label: Self-adjoint operator

anchor.snippet: A (formally) self-adjoint operator is an operator equal to its own (formal) adjoint

annotations.note: Formal self-adjointness for possibly unbounded operators is `LinearMap.IsSymmetric`; bounded case is `IsSelfAdjoint`.

annotations.label: Adjoint in several variables

anchor.snippet: the adjoint of P is defined in

annotations.note: Generic adjoint of an operator on a Hilbert space exists, but no multi-index formula P* = Σ (−1)^|α| D^α (ȳ_α ·) for differential operators.

annotations.label: Sturm–Liouville operator

anchor.snippet: The Sturm–Liouville operator is a well-known example of a formal self-adjoint operator

annotations.note: Grep returns no Sturm–Liouville file in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linearity of differentiation

anchor.snippet: Differentiation is linear

annotations.note: Linearity is captured by `deriv_add`/`fderiv_add` and the corresponding scalar-multiplication lemmas in `Analysis.Calculus.Deriv`.

annotations.label: Composition of differential operators

anchor.snippet: We may also compose differential operators by the rule

annotations.note: Mathlib has chain-rule composition (`deriv.comp`, `fderiv.comp`) but does not formalize composition as an algebra operation on differential operators.

annotations.label: Constant-coefficient subring is commutative

anchor.snippet: The subring of operators that are polynomials in D with constant coefficients is, by contrast, commutative

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the subring of constant-coefficient differential operators or its commutativity.

annotations.label: Ring of univariate polynomial differential operators

anchor.section: Ring of univariate polynomial differential operators
anchor.snippet: the ring of univariate polynomial differential operators over R is the quotient ring

annotations.note: No Weyl algebra A₁(R) / ring of univariate polynomial differential operators is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Univariate ring is non-commutative simple

anchor.section: Ring of univariate polynomial differential operators
anchor.snippet: This is a non-commutative simple ring

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks the underlying object, so simplicity of A₁ is unstated.

annotations.label: Ring of multivariate polynomial differential operators

anchor.section: Ring of multivariate polynomial differential operators
anchor.snippet: the ring of multivariate polynomial differential operators over R is the quotient ring

annotations.note: No multivariate Weyl algebra Aₙ(R) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multivariate ring is non-commutative simple

anchor.section: Ring of multivariate polynomial differential operators
anchor.snippet: This is a non-commutative simple ring

annotations.note: Without the multivariate Weyl algebra defined, the simplicity claim is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: k-th order linear differential operator via jet bundle

anchor.snippet: is said to be a k th-order linear differential operator if it factors through the jet bundle

annotations.note: Mathlib only mentions jet bundles informally in a doc-string and does not define `J^k E` or factorization through it.

annotations.label: Peetre theorem

anchor.snippet: A foundational result is the Peetre theorem

annotations.note: Grep for `Peetre` returns no Mathlib hits.

annotations.label: Algebraic characterization of differential operator

anchor.snippet: an R -linear map P is a k th-order linear differential operator

annotations.note: The inductive Grothendieck-style characterization via iterated commutators is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Differential operator of infinite order

anchor.section: A differential operator of infinite order
anchor.snippet: A differential operator of infinite order is (roughly) a differential operator whose total symbol is a power series

annotations.note: No infinite-order differential operator object is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invariant differential operator

anchor.snippet: An invariant differential operator is a differential operator that is also an invariant operator

annotations.note: Grep for `invariant differential` produces no hits in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bidifferential operator

anchor.snippet: is called a bidifferential operator

annotations.note: No notion of bidifferential operator is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Microdifferential operator

anchor.snippet: A microdifferential operator is a type of operator on an open subset of a cotangent bundle

annotations.note: Grep for `microdifferential` returns no Mathlib hits.
